follow_up_question: What makes Gorillaz unique as a band?

title: Going Up! The First Otis Elevator
story: Ding! The doors slide open. In 1857, Elisha Otis changed how we move through tall buildings. On March 23, he installed his first elevator at 488 Broadway in New York City. This wasn't just any lift - Otis invented a special safety brake. If the ropes broke, the elevator wouldn't fall. His invention made skyscrapers possible and cities grew up, up, up!
activity_question: What did Elisha Otis invent for his elevator?
follow_up_question: How do you think cities might look different today if elevators hadn't been invented?

title: Halley's Comet Blazes Across Medieval Skies
story: A fiery visitor streaked through the night sky in 1066, amazing people across Europe. This celestial spectacle was Halley's Comet, making its 18th recorded close approach to the Sun on March 23. The comet's appearance was seen as an omen by many, including King Harold of England, who would face invasion later that year.
activity_question: What celestial object amazed people across Europe in 1066?
bonus_fact: Halley's Comet returns to Earth's view about every 75-76 years. Its next visit will be in 2061!

title: Scottish Pioneers Sail to New Zealand
story: Brave Scottish settlers embarked on a grand adventure in 1848. The ship John Wickliffe sailed across vast oceans, finally reaching Port Chalmers on March 23. These pioneers were the first to arrive in what would become Dunedin, New Zealand. Their journey marked the founding of Otago province, a new home far from Scotland's shores.
activity_question: What was the name of the ship that brought the Scottish settlers?
follow_up_question: Why do you think these settlers chose to travel so far from their homeland?

title: Weather Watchers Unite!
story: Clouds, rain, and sunshine got a global team in 1950! The United Nations created the World Meteorological Organization on March 23. This group helps countries share weather info and work together on climate challenges. From predicting storms to studying long-term changes, these weather experts keep us informed about our ever-changing skies.
activity_question: What does the World Meteorological Organization help countries do?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for countries to work together on weather and climate issues?

title: Immaculata's Slam Dunk Victory
story: Swish! The basketball sailed through the hoop as Immaculata College clinched a thrilling victory. On March 23, 1974, they defeated Mississippi College 68-53 in the AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament. The intense match took place in Manhattan, Kansas, showcasing the growing excitement around women's college basketball.
activity_question: Where did the basketball tournament take place?

title: Math Whiz Wins 'Nobel' of Numbers
story: Imagine solving puzzles about the shapes of the universe! That's what American mathematician Dennis Sullivan does. His amazing work on topography earned him the Abel Prize in 2022. This award is like the Nobel Prize, but for math geniuses. On March 23, Sullivan joined the ranks of the world's top problem-solvers, showing that math can unlock cosmic mysteries!
activity_question: What prize did Dennis Sullivan win for his work in mathematics?
follow_up_question: What area of math did Sullivan work on?

title: Utah Takes Bold Step for Kids Online
story: Utah made history in 2023 by becoming the first state to set rules for how kids use social media. The new law creates an overnight curfew and requires parents to give permission before their children can join platforms. This groundbreaking move aims to protect young people from potential online dangers and promote healthier digital habits.
activity_question: What does the new Utah law require for kids using social media?
activity_choices: An overnight curfew and parental permission
A daily time limit of one hour
activity_answer: An overnight curfew and parental permission
follow_up_question: Do you think these rules will help kids have a better relationship with social media?

title: A Hero Returns to Fight for Freedom
story: General Tadeusz Kościuszko sneaked back into Kraków, Poland in 1794. He came to lead a brave uprising against Russian and Prussian control. On March 23, Kościuszko rallied the people to fight for their country's independence. His return sparked hope for a free Poland.
activity_question: Who returned to Kraków to lead an uprising?
follow_up_question: Why do you think Kościuszko's return gave people hope?

title: The King's Last Tour Begins
story: Fans screamed with excitement as Elvis Presley took the stage on March 23, 1977. Little did they know, this was the start of the King of Rock and Roll's final concert tour. Elvis thrilled audiences with his iconic voice and moves, but his health was declining. Sadly, he passed away just a few months later, making these performances even more special.
activity_question: What did Elvis Presley begin on March 23, 1977?
bonus_fact: Elvis was known for his love of peanut butter and banana sandwiches.
